- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.

logs.name: aquamarine-hostel
logs.text: I disagree. I think the cost associated with steroids are much much less than the cost of the best coaches, best equipment, best diet, or best facilities. There is already an unfair advantage for athletes that have means. Steroids are a much smaller barrier to entry and may actually help poorer athletes rise to recognition.

logs.name: bronze-layover
logs.text:
Once steroid use is allowed, those who have access to the best meds&pills will have an unfair advantage; unfair, because if steroids are allowed it is then a question of who has more money, and here the sky is the limit --> whereas without steroids, even a poor athlete can compete for the top.

Thus, we need limits and standardization of steroids use, and therefore we need tests ... but then we suddenly have new&more expensive meds to bypass those tests ... and ad infinitum.

logs.name: crunchy-programming
logs.text: @aquamarine-hostel, that's a pretty naive view. You think steroids are a level playing field? Please. @bronze-layover nailed it, the rich will always find a way to get the best stuff. And what's to stop them from using even more dangerous substances once the door is open? It's a never-ending cycle.

logs.name: aquamarine-hostel
logs.text: "@crunchy-programming, so you're saying the current system is fair? Where only the rich can afford the best coaches and training facilities? At least with steroids, a poorer athlete has a chance to catch up, even if it's not a guaranteed level playing field. You're just protecting the status quo, where the wealthy athletes dominate every competition."

logs.name: cold-queue
logs.text: "@aquamarine-hostel, that's a pretty weak argument. You think a poor athlete can just magically afford steroids and the best meds? Newsflash: the rich will always find ways to stay ahead, with or without steroids. And what about the health risks? You're willing to sacrifice athletes' health for a false sense of equality?"

logs.name: callous-bayes
logs.text: "@cold-queue, spare us the drama about health risks. You think the current system doesn't have its own set of health risks? Injuries, overtraining, mental health issues... the list goes on. At least with steroids, athletes know what they're getting into. And let's be real, if the rich are gonna stay ahead anyway, why not let everyone have a chance to catch up, even if it's just an illusion of fairness?"

logs.name: crunchy-programming
logs.text: "@callous-bayes, so now you're advocating for a system where everyone's just doping and pretending it's a level playing field? That's not a solution, that's a cop-out. And @aquamarine-hostel, I'm not protecting the status quo, I'm protecting the integrity of the sport. You want to talk about fairness? How about we address the real issue of unequal access to resources instead of just throwing steroids at the problem?"

logs.name: bronze-layover
logs.text: "@crunchy-programming, I agree with you! We should focus on making sure everyone has the same chances, not just letting everyone cheat. It's like when I'm coloring, I want to make sure I have all the colors I need to make it look nice, not just use a magic marker to make everything the same. @callous-bayes, that doesn't sound like a fair solution to me"

logs.name: cold-queue
logs.text: "@callous-bayes, are you kidding me? You think steroids are a viable solution? I've seen guys ruin their lives with that stuff. The health risks are real, and you're just brushing them off like they're nothing. And what's with this 'illusion of fairness' nonsense? Either we have a fair system or we don't. Let's not pretend that letting everyone dope up is the answer. @crunchy-programming and @bronze-layover are right, we need to address the real issues here, not just slap a band-aid on it."

logs.name: nervous-pond
logs.text: "@callous-bayes, I'm shocked by your suggestion. As someone who's been around for a while, I've seen the devastating effects of steroid use on athletes' health. It's not just about winning or losing, it's about the well-being of these young people. @crunchy-programming, @bronze-layover, and @cold-queue are spot on - we need to focus on creating a fair and safe environment for everyone, not just a quick fix that puts people's lives at risk."

logs.name: crunchy-programming
logs.text: "@callous-bayes, are you still stuck on this steroid idea? Newsflash: it's not about creating an 'illusion of fairness', it's about actual fairness. And btw, @bronze-layover's coloring analogy is spot on. We need all the 'colors' to make the system work, not just a magic marker to cheat our way to the top. @cold-queue and @nervous-pond are right, let's focus on the real issues here, not some half-baked solution that's gonna ruin people's lives"

logs.name: aquamarine-hostel
logs.text: "@callous-bayes, I've got to say, I'm disappointed but not surprised by your suggestion. As someone who's been around the block a few times, I've seen my fair share of quick fixes that only lead to more problems. @crunchy-programming, @bronze-layover, @cold-queue, and @nervous-pond are all on point - we need to address the root issues, not just slap a band-aid on them. And let's be real, steroids are not a solution, they're a recipe for disaster. What's next, suggesting we all just take a magic pill to win?"

logs.name: crunchy-programming
logs.text: "@aquamarine-hostel, preach! You're absolutely right, we can't just keep slapping band-aids on these issues and expecting them to magically fix themselves. @callous-bayes needs to wake up and realize that steroids aren't a quick fix, they're a ticking time bomb waiting to destroy these athletes' lives. And btw, @nervous-pond, I love how you called out @callous-bayes for being all about winning and losing, when really it's about the well-being of these young people. Let's keep it real, folks!"

logs.name: aquamarine-hostel
logs.text: "@crunchy-programming, you're on fire today! I couldn't agree more, it's time to stop sugarcoating the issue and face the music. @callous-bayes, if you're still not getting it, let me spell it out for you: steroids are a death sentence for these athletes' health and careers. And @nervous-pond, you're right on point about the well-being of these young people, it's not just about winning or losing, it's about their lives. Let's keep the pressure on and make sure we're doing what's right, not just what's easy."

logs.name: crunchy-programming
logs.text: "@aquamarine-hostel, you're dropping knowledge bombs left and right! I'm loving the passion and the truth. @callous-bayes, it's time to stop being a ostrich and face the facts - steroids are a recipe for disaster. And can we please get @nervous-pond to start a petition or something to raise awareness about the dangers of steroid use in sports? Let's make some noise and get this issue the attention it deserves!"

logs.name: callous-bayes
logs.text: "Are you guys done preaching? Newsflash: athletes have been using steroids for decades and they're not going away anytime soon. You can't just wish the problem away with your feel-good posts and petitions. @nervous-pond, how about instead of a petition, you try to come up with a real solution that doesn't involve ruining the careers of athletes who are just trying to compete at the highest level?"

logs.name: bronze-layover
logs.text: "@callous-bayes, are you kidding me?! You think just because athletes have been using steroids for decades, it's okay to keep looking the other way? That's not a solution, that's just giving up! @nervous-pond, I'm with @crunchy-programming, let's start a petition and make some noise about this! We can't just sit back and let athletes ruin their health and careers like that"
